exactSegmentDerivativeCenters.theoremName: adjoint Green endpoint active selection with exact segment derivatives

radiusModel.interpretation: The rank conclusion is rigorous only if these radii enclose the exact endpoint fundamental-matrix map.

conditionalBallFullRowRankStatus.label: conditional ball full active row rank
conditionalBallFullRowRankStatus.reason: Weyl perturbation gives sigma_min(M_exact)>=sigma_min(M0)-delta for the supplied endpoint-map ball.

rigorousEndpointMapFullRowRankStatus.label: rigorous endpoint map full active row rank
rigorousEndpointMapFullRowRankStatus.reason: Closed only if the supplied endpoint-map ball is a rigorous interval enclosure of the exact fundamental-matrix endpoint map.

kernelMStarZeroStatus.label: ker M^* is zero
kernelMStarZeroStatus.reason: If the rigorous full-row-rank status is closed, then the left Fredholm obstruction space is trivial and endpoint compatibility is vacuous.

moorePenroseEndpointBoundStatus.label: Moore-Penrose endpoint bound
moorePenroseEndpointBoundStatus.reason: On the supplied ball, ||M^+|| <= 1/(sigma_min(M0)-delta).  This becomes a rigorous continuum bound only with a rigorous endpoint map enclosure.

proof: If this lower bound is positive, M_exact has full active row rank.
Full active row rank implies ker(M^*)={0}, so the endpoint Fredholm compatibility condition is vacuous.
sampledVariantRankBall.remainingAnalyticGap: Produce a rigorous interval enclosure for the exact endpoint fundamental-matrix map with Frobenius radius below the displayed maxAllowedFrobeniusRadiusForRank.

rigorousEndpointMapFullRowRankStatus.reason: Closed only if the supplied endpoint-map ball is a rigorous interval enclosure of the exact fundamental-matrix endpoint map.

kernelMStarZeroStatus.label: ker M^* is zero
kernelMStarZeroStatus.reason: If the rigorous full-row-rank status is closed, then the left Fredholm obstruction space is trivial and endpoint compatibility is vacuous.

moorePenroseEndpointBoundStatus.reason: On the supplied ball, ||M^+|| <= 1/(sigma_min(M0)-delta).  This becomes a rigorous continuum bound only with a rigorous endpoint map enclosure.

exactSegmentVariantRankBall.remainingAnalyticGap: Produce a rigorous interval enclosure for the exact endpoint fundamental-matrix map with Frobenius radius below the displayed maxAllowedFrobeniusRadiusForRank.

stabilityStatus.label: Chebyshev endpoint center stability
stabilityStatus.reason: Closed only if successive Chebyshev endpoint maps differ by less than the rank margin.  This is still a numerical center test, not an interval proof.

stabilityStatus.label: Chebyshev endpoint center stability
stabilityStatus.reason: Closed only if successive Chebyshev endpoint maps differ by less than the rank margin.  This is still a numerical center test, not an interval proof.

stabilityStatus.label: Magnus endpoint center stability
stabilityStatus.reason: Closed only if successive Magnus endpoint maps differ by less than the rank margin.  This is a numerical center test, not an interval proof.

frobeniusStabilityStatus.label: row-flow Frobenius stability
frobeniusStabilityStatus.reason: Closed if successive row-flow endpoint maps differ below the old rank margin.

minorStabilityStatus.label: row-flow best-minor stability
minorStabilityStatus.reason: Closed if the same best 2x2 minor persists and its relative change is below 25 percent across refinements.

frobeniusStabilityStatus.label: row-flow Frobenius stability
frobeniusStabilityStatus.reason: Closed if successive row-flow endpoint maps differ below the old rank margin.

minorStabilityStatus.label: row-flow best-minor stability
minorStabilityStatus.reason: Closed if the same best 2x2 minor persists and its relative change is below 25 percent across refinements.

persistentChartNoncollapseStatus.label: persistent affine Grassmann chart identified
persistentChartNoncollapseStatus.reason: Closed at the numerical-center level if one Pluecker coordinate stays uniformly away from zero across all recorded orders.

tailProjectiveStabilityStatus.label: tail projective Grassmann stability
tailProjectiveStabilityStatus.reason: Closed at the numerical-center level if the latest normalized two-plane comparisons are below the configured projective distance tolerance.

conditionalChartBallTheoremStatus.label: conditional chart-ball noncollapse theorem
conditionalChartBallTheoremStatus.reason: If an exact interval/Riccati computation encloses the normalized Pluecker vector inside the certified projective ball, then p_01 is nonzero and the stronger threshold margin holds.

exactFlowEnclosureStatus.label: exact endpoint Riccati/Pluecker flow enclosure
exactFlowEnclosureStatus.reason: Open.  This script supplies the chart-ball target and interval algebra, but it does not yet enclose the exact ODE flow.

grassmannChartBallCertificate.conclusion: The persistent chart has large margin.  A projective enclosure radius below 0.464332 proves |p_hat_01| >= 0.5; the latest observed projective distance is 0.0292018.
grassmannChartBallCertificate.nextTarget: Integrate the Riccati equations for the chart ratios p_J/p_01 with ball arithmetic and prove the exact projective radius is below the certified radius.

exteriorEquationStatus.label: exact exterior-square equation
exteriorEquationStatus.reason: For row flow M'=-MA, the Pluecker vector satisfies p'=B(s)p with B constructed by the exterior-square formula in this certificate.

riccatiChartEquationStatus.label: exact Riccati chart equation
riccatiChartEquationStatus.reason: In the chart p_01 != 0, ratios z_J=p_J/p_01 satisfy the displayed Riccati equation z'=Bp/p_01-z(Bp)_01/p_01.

chartTubeNoncollapseStatus.label: chart tube noncollapse
chartTubeNoncollapseStatus.reason: The certified projective tube lies within the chart-ball radius needed for |p_hat_01| >= 1/2.

empiricalRefinementInsideTubeStatus.label: empirical refinement inside chart tube
empiricalRefinementInsideTubeStatus.reason: The latest observed 9->11 projective movement, inflated by a factor four, is inside the certified projective radius. This is evidence, not the exact residual proof.

validatedExactFlowEnclosureStatus.label: validated exact endpoint Riccati flow enclosure
validatedExactFlowEnclosureStatus.reason: Open.  The ODE and chart tube are now explicit.  To close this, prove a residual or interval-integration bound below the listed projective radius budget.

riccatiFlowEnclosure.conclusion: The exact exterior/Riccati equation has been constructed in the persistent p_01 chart.  The chart tube has large noncollapse margin; the remaining proof obligation is a validated residual or interval integration bound for the exact endpoint BVP flow.
riccatiFlowEnclosure.nextTarget: Use a Chebyshev/Krawczyk or interval Taylor solve for the Riccati BVP and prove the projective enclosure radius is below the certified chart-ball radius.

linearCollocationKrawczykStatus.label: linear row-flow Krawczyk theorem
linearCollocationKrawczykStatus.reason: The finite collocation systems have explicit inverse norms. If scaled companion-matrix and endpoint-row interval radii are below the reported capacities, the endpoint map remains inside the persistent Pluecker chart threshold.

exactCoefficientEnclosureStatus.label: exact coefficient and boundary interval enclosure
exactCoefficientEnclosureStatus.reason: Open.  The Krawczyk radii are now quantified, but the exact confluent trace-derivative coefficients and boundary rows still need interval/ball enclosures below these radii.

riccatiKrawczykCollocation.conclusion: The Krawczyk/collocation proof has been reduced to finite coefficient and boundary-row interval radii.  This avoids the useless raw Riccati Gronwall bound, but exact coefficient balls are still required to close the theorem.
riccatiKrawczykCollocation.nextTarget: Build interval balls for the scaled companion matrices and active endpoint boundary rows, then compare their radii with the Krawczyk capacities reported here.

coefficientBallEnclosure.theoremName: endpoint coefficient and boundary-row ball enclosure

scaledCompanionEnclosureStatus.label: scaled companion matrix ball below Krawczyk capacity
scaledCompanionEnclosureStatus.reason: Closed when the safety-inflated refinement ball for every scaled companion entry is below the Krawczyk coefficient radius capacity.

boundaryRowEnclosureStatus.label: active endpoint boundary-row ball below Krawczyk capacity
boundaryRowEnclosureStatus.reason: Closed when the safety-inflated refinement ball for every active endpoint boundary row entry is below the Krawczyk boundary radius capacity.

validatedKrawczykCoefficientInputStatus.label: validated Krawczyk coefficient input
validatedKrawczykCoefficientInputStatus.reason: This closes the finite coefficient-input layer conditional on the stated sample/refinement ball enclosure model.  A fully formal proof would replace this model with interval quadrature tail bounds for the confluent integral.

coefficientBallEnclosure.conclusion: The exact objects needed by the Krawczyk proof are now compared against stricter confluent computations.  Passing radii mean the scaled companion matrices and active endpoint rows are inside the Krawczyk budgets.
coefficientBallEnclosure.nextTarget: If this passes, state the endpoint full-rank theorem conditional on the validated confluent quadrature enclosure; if it fails, increase dps/matrix order or derive analytic quadrature tails.

sampledCenterStabilityStatus.label: sampled finite-difference endpoint center stability
sampledCenterStabilityStatus.reason: Stable only if mesh-refinement endpoint maps remain within a small constant factor.  They currently vary by many orders of magnitude.

exactSegmentCenterStabilityStatus.label: exact-segment derivative endpoint center stability
exactSegmentCenterStabilityStatus.reason: Stable only if exact-derivative segment refinements remain within a small constant factor.  Current piecewise-constant propagation still varies by many orders of magnitude.

rankBallCenterReadyStatus.label: rank-ball center ready
rankBallCenterReadyStatus.reason: A rank-ball proof needs a stable center before interval radii can be meaningful.  Current sampled and exact-segment centers are not stable enough; Chebyshev centers are tracked separately.

chebyshevCenterStabilityStatus.label: Chebyshev endpoint center stability
chebyshevCenterStabilityStatus.reason: Closed only if all recorded Chebyshev refinement differences fall below the rank margin.

magnusCenterStabilityStatus.label: Magnus endpoint center stability
magnusCenterStabilityStatus.reason: Closed only if all recorded Magnus refinement differences fall below the rank margin.

rowFlowMinorStabilityStatus.label: adjoint row-flow minor stability
rowFlowMinorStabilityStatus.reason: Closed only if the same transported-row 2x2 minor persists and changes by less than 25 percent across refinements.

grassmannProjectiveStabilityStatus.label: normalized Grassmann projective stability
grassmannProjectiveStabilityStatus.reason: Closed at the numerical-center level if a persistent Pluecker chart stays away from zero and the tail projective comparisons are below tolerance.  This is not yet an interval enclosure of the exact endpoint flow.

grassmannChartBallStatus.label: persistent Pluecker chart ball theorem
grassmannChartBallStatus.reason: Closed only as interval algebra: if the exact normalized Pluecker vector is enclosed in the stated projective ball, then the chart minor stays bounded away from zero.

riccatiFlowEquationStatus.label: exact exterior/Riccati flow equation and tube
riccatiFlowEquationStatus.reason: Closed when the exterior-square ODE, Riccati chart equation, and chart-tube noncollapse check are all recorded.  This still does not prove the exact endpoint BVP flow lies in the tube.

riccatiKrawczykCollocationStatus.label: Riccati-chart Krawczyk collocation reduction
riccatiKrawczykCollocationStatus.reason: Closed as a finite interval-collocation reduction.  Exact coefficient and endpoint-row interval balls still need to be proved below the reported Krawczyk capacities.

coefficientBallEnclosureStatus.label: coefficient and boundary-row ball below Krawczyk capacity
coefficientBallEnclosureStatus.reason: Closed under the stated sample/refinement ball model when the scaled companion and active boundary-row radii are below the Krawczyk capacities.

conclusion: The desired endpoint-map rank proof cannot be closed from the current sampled-flow center.  Chebyshev collocation greatly improves the scale and gives rank-two centers, but the recorded 5->7 and 7->9 differences remain above the rank margin.  The two-point Magnus step is worse, blowing up under 2->4 refinement.  Adjoint row-flow propagation identifies the same best minor across refinements, but the raw determinant is not stable.  The normalized exterior/Grassmann diagnostic removes the raw scale and finds a persistent affine chart.  The chart-ball algebra now gives the exact projective radius needed for noncollapse.  The exterior/Riccati equations have been constructed, the Krawczyk/collocation reduction gives explicit coefficient and endpoint-row radius budgets, and the finite sample-ball enclosure is below those budgets with large slack. The last numerical rigor item is replacing the sample-ball model with analytic interval quadrature tails for the confluent integrals, or returning to obstruction annihilation.
nextTarget: Replace the coefficient sample-ball model with formal interval quadrature/tail bounds for the confluent integrals.
